commands.review_summary:
The PR switches the default OpenClaw gateway package from the source/pnpm build to an npm-shrinkwrap-backed package, updates pin automation and CI/Garnix coverage, revises docs, adds performance audit tooling, and carries runtime-plugin materialization/API changes.

Reproducibility: yes. for the main patch defect by source inspection: the published npm package has unbundled runtime dependencies, while the new install script copies only the package root into the output. I did not run a build because this is a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This collaborator draft needs maintainer decisions on packaging source of truth, plugin API scope, CI gate coverage, and supply-chain/security acceptance before any automated repair lane is appropriate.

target.title: Default gateway to npm shrinkwrap
target.body:
for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels

# Human written summary:
The intent of this change is, as written by a human:
> clean elegant install in nix of npm package; we have many hacks from dirty packaging, we want to clean them up as much as possible to reduce total build time (both in CI and locally) and make packaging less fragile and more predictable; using tools like shrinkwrap if we can; and other optimizations as necessary. fewer dependencies in the tree is also good, faster build time is good, smaller is good, etc, you know the drill.

_The rest of this PR was written by GPT-5 Codex, running in the Codex desktop harness._

# Changes
- Builds the stable `openclaw-gateway` from the published `openclaw` npm package when `gatewayNpmDepsHash` and `acpxNpmDepsHash` are present.
- Keeps the source/pnpm builder as the explicit fallback for `gatewaySrc` and dogfood-style source pins.
- Uses lockfile-backed npm wrapper builds for `openclaw` and `@openclaw/acpx`, with version assertions against `sourceInfo.releaseVersion`.
- Localizes the bundled ACPX compatibility package under the npm gateway derivation instead of exporting `openclaw-bundled-acpx` as a top-level package.
- Removes the old source-check derivation, source-check helper scripts, and stable-pin `pnpmDepsHash` refresh from the default release-update path.
- Narrows CI/Garnix to the stable npm package surface; dogfood remains an explicit package/check output but no longer inflates the default aggregate.
- Adds `maintainers/performance-audit.md` as an append-only, commit-keyed metric ledger with baseline/measured provenance, exact commands, and current measurements.
- Aligns README and maintainer docs with the npm-shrinkwrapped gateway default.

# Why
Upstream `openclaw@2026.6.1` publishes `npm-shrinkwrap.json`. npm documents shrinkwrap as a publishable lockfile, and nixpkgs documents `buildNpmPackage`/`npmDepsHash` as the fixed-output npm dependency cache path. This PR uses that shape for the default package and removes the old source-build validation work from the normal stable pin flow.

Docs checked:
- https://nixos.org/manual/nixpkgs/stable/#javascript
- https://docs.npmjs.com/files/package-lock.json/
- https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v11/commands/npm-shrinkwrap

# Measured Impact
Measured on 2026-06-05. Size baselines compare the old source-build gateway output to the npm gateway output. Workflow/check baselines compare the earlier draft PR shape before the simplification pass.

| Metric | Before | After | Change |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| `openclaw-gateway` closure | 2,273,877,888 bytes | 915,457,000 bytes | 59.7% smaller |
| `openclaw` closure | 3,215,431,032 bytes | 1,857,010,136 bytes | 42.2% smaller |
| gateway output size | 2.1G | 727M | 66.2% smaller |
| package manifests | 1,452 | 584 | 59.8% fewer |
| files under `lib/openclaw` | 97,909 | 34,053 | 65.2% fewer |
| Garnix targets | 83 | 5 | 94.0% fewer |
| Darwin check attrs | 13 | 11 | 15.4% fewer |
| Linux check attrs | 14 | 12 | 14.3% fewer |
| workflow hardcoded npm wrapper paths | 20 | 0 | removed |
| top-level ACPX package outputs | 1 | 0 | removed |

Build-time proof:
- New default gateway forced rebuild previously succeeded in `51.03s`.
- Old source-builder forced rebuild previously ran `399.37s` and then hit a Nix determinism failure.
- Same-version stable pin apply now completes in `80.28s`; it no longer refreshes/builds the old source pnpm dependency graph.
- Cached CI aggregate after the default-surface cleanup: `42.56s` on Darwin and `43.58s` on Linux. Later reruns included Nix auto-GC and are recorded separately in `maintainers/performance-audit.md`.

# Proof
Local commands run on the current head:

- `bash -n scripts/update-pins.sh nix/scripts/check-package-contents.sh nix/scripts/openclaw-gateway-npm-install.sh nix/scripts/openclaw-bundled-acpx-install.sh`
- `node --check nix/scripts/patch-openclaw-npm-dist.mjs`
- `node --check nix/scripts/update-openclaw-runtime-plugin-locks.mjs`
- `ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file("garnix.yaml"); YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/ci.yml"); YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/pin-stable-openclaw-version.yml")'`
- `GITHUB_ACTIONS=true /usr/bin/time -p scripts/update-pins.sh apply v2026.6.1 2e08f0f4221f522b60423ed6ffd83427942b28de v2026.6.1 https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/download/v2026.6.1/OpenClaw-2026.6.1.zip`
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link --print-out-paths .#checks.aarch64-darwin.ci`
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link --print-out-paths .#checks.x86_64-linux.ci`
- `git diff --cached --check`

# Plugin PR / Stacking
Plugins are intentionally out of scope for this PR. PR #99 owns runtime plugin shrinkwrap/materialization. This PR avoids changing the `programs.openclaw.runtimePlugins` interface and does not replace the plugin pipeline.

The one ACPX bridge kept here is gateway-local because the published `openclaw@2026.6.1` npm package does not include ACPX runtime files; it only includes the ACPX reference doc. That keeps the current gateway contract without pulling PR #99's plugin builder internals into this package flip.

# Risks
- The default gateway source changes from source/pnpm output to the published npm package, so runtime layout and upgrade behavior remain the main compatibility review points.
- The default stable source pin no longer carries `pnpmDepsHash`; source builds still work through dogfood/source pins that carry pnpm hashes or explicit `gatewaySrc` + `pnpmDepsHash`.
- The npm package does not yet make ACPX disappear as a gateway-local concern. That should converge with the plugin shrinkwrap work later, not in this PR.

# Follow-ups
- Latest remote GitHub Actions and Garnix checks are green on commit `0bbc4080b`.
- After PR #99 lands, re-check whether the gateway-local ACPX bridge can be retired without creating a cycle between `openclaw-gateway` and runtime plugin packages.
- Keep `maintainers/performance-audit.md` current when packaging changes move these metrics.
